titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Argentina ( (Armour) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina ( Armour ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Buenos Aires , December 20, 1943—3 p.m.
[Received December 20—2:54 p.m.]

div.p:
2921. EspilFelipe A. Espil, Argentine Ambassador to the United States. tells me when news of the resolution referred to [in] Department’s 1895, December 14, 9 p.m.Not printed; for substance, see telegram No. 1017, December 3, 4 p.m., from the Ambassador in Uruguay, supra. reached Foreign Office here they were very angry, in fact were inclined not to appoint successor to Achaval, recently designated Minister to Switzerland. Espil who was apparently consulted recommended against such a stand, on ground that Pan American Union, to which resolution has been referred, would then be in a position to designate a delegate from some other country leaving Argentine unrepresented. This, he told them, would, he felt, be a serious mistake. While Espil personally understands reasons prompting Committee in its action, he doubts whether Pan American Union has power to act in sense recommended. Espil does not know
whether his Government will act on his recommendation, but he is working quietly to have Ricardo BungeDirector of the Division of American Political Affairs of the Argentine Foreign Office. designated as Achaval’s successor.
